content.plain_english: This week your child is meeting halves and quarters — splitting a shape, an object or a small group of things into equal parts.
content.in_school: Children fold paper to make halves, then quarters, and stick them back together to see they make a whole. They share groups of objects (4 grapes between 2 friends, 8 grapes between 4 friends) to find a half or a quarter of a quantity.
content.home_activity: At lunch, hand your child an apple. Ask them to cut it in half. How many halves make a whole? Then cut a sandwich into quarters. How many quarters in a half? Three minutes, real food.
dinner_table_questions: If we cut the pizza into 2 equal slices, what is each slice called?
Half of 8 is 4. So what is half of 6?
Can you cut your toast into four equal pieces?

key_vocabulary.definition: The full thing before it is cut up.
One of two equal parts. Written as 1/2.
One of four equal parts. Written as 1/4.
Parts that are exactly the same size.

example_questions: If a chocolate bar has 8 squares and you eat a quarter, how many squares?
Show me a piece of paper folded into 4 equal parts.
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. Halves are best friends with counting in 2s — half of 10 is 5, half of 8 is 4. Spot the pattern.
